text: Many real-world applications, such as computational biology, information retrieval, and natural language processing, generate large quantities of structured data. Relational learning algorithms are therefore essential for the analysis of this data. We introduce a new neural network architecture for relational learning that can scale to large multi-relational graphs and learn useful representations of the data. Our approach can learn to embed multi-relational graphs into a flexible vector space, where the data is preserved and augmented. The network is trained so that it assigns high probability to legitimate graph components. We show that the approach is competitive with state-of-the-art approaches for link prediction tasks on several standard datasets from the literature, as well as on data from a real-world knowledge base. We also show that the approach can be used to perform word-sense disambiguation in open-text semantic parsing, where the goal is to learn to assign a structured meaning representation to almost any sentence of free text. We demonstrate that the approach can scale to datasets with tens of thousands of nodes and thousands of relation types.
headline: Fully Human Written
